description:
After each k inlining in inline_single_use_conts_once, re-type only the affected caller specs instead of letting the caller do a full type_module pass.

## What to change

In inline_single_use_conts_once(m, module_types: &mut ModuleTypes):

After inlining k into caller:
1. For each spec key (caller_id, descrs) in module_types.specs, call:
     type_fn(caller_fn, m, Some(&descrs)) → new FnTypes
   and update module_types.specs entry.
2. Remove all entries (k_id, _) from module_types.specs:
     module_types.specs.retain(|(fid, _), _| *fid != k_id)

The var_map returned by alpha_rename is no longer needed for types (the re-type via type_fn handles it), but it should still be verified correct for block/var id consistency.

## Signature change

```rust
pub fn inline_single_use_conts(m: &mut Module, module_types: &mut ModuleTypes)
```

The inner loop in inline_single_use_conts threads the &mut ModuleTypes into inline_single_use_conts_once.

## Acceptance
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes.
- All tests pass.
- One commit referencing fz-ty1.pip.1.
title: fz-ty1.pip.1: surgical type refresh in inline_single_use_conts_once
